UserAccount.title: UserAccount
UserAccount.description: Represents a user account in InvoiceFlow, tracking usage metrics, subscription status, and basic profile information. It does not store authentication credentials.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserAccount entity.

externalAuthId.description: Identifier provided by the external authentication system (e.g., Google, Apple) for the user.

email.description: Email address of the user, typically obtained from the authentication provider.

displayName.description: Display name of the user, typically obtained from the authentication provider.

freeDownloadsUsed.description: Number of invoices/estimates downloaded by the user without logging in (up to 2 allowed).

loggedInDownloadsUsed.description: Number of invoices/estimates downloaded by the user after logging in (up to 3 additional downloads for free users).

donationPromptCount.description: Counts how many times the user has been presented with the 'Buy Me a Coffee' donation prompt.

donationDeclineCount.description: Counts how many times the user has declined the donation prompt (up to 2 declines allowed before donation is required).

isPremiumSubscriber.description: Indicates if the user currently has an active premium subscription.

subscriptionId.description: Reference to the active Subscription entity for this user, if they are a premium subscriber. (Relationship: Subscription 1:1 UserAccount)

invoiceNumberCounter.description: The next sequential invoice number to be used for this user's invoices, primarily for premium subscribers with automatic numbering.

isAdmin.description: Indicates if the user has administrative privileges, typically for accessing the CMS backend.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account record was last updated.

UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores user-specific default preferences and sender information for invoices and estimates.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserAccount entity this profile belongs to. (Relationship: UserAccount 1:1 UserProfile)

defaultCompanyName.description: The user's default company name to appear on invoices and estimates.

defaultAddressLine1.description: The user's default primary address line for invoices and estimates.

defaultAddressLine2.description: The user's default secondary address line for invoices and estimates.

defaultCity.description: The user's default city for invoices and estimates.

defaultStateProvince.description: The user's default state or province for invoices and estimates.

defaultPostalCode.description: The user's default postal code for invoices and estimates.

defaultCountry.description: The user's default country for invoices and estimates.

defaultEmail.description: The user's default email address for invoices and estimates.

defaultPhone.description: The user's default phone number for invoices and estimates.

defaultTaxRate.description: The user's default tax rate percentage to apply to invoices and estimates.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Client.title: Client
Client.description: Represents a client or customer to whom invoices and estimates are issued.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Client entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserAccount entity that owns this client record. (Relationship: UserAccount 1:N Client)

name.description: Full name or company name of the client.

billingAddressLine1.description: Primary address line of the client's billing address.

billingAddressLine2.description: Secondary address line of the client's billing address.

billingCity.description: City of the client's billing address.

billingStateProvince.description: State or province of the client's billing address.

billingPostalCode.description: Postal code of the client's billing address.

billingCountry.description: Country of the client's billing address.

shippingAddressLine1.description: Primary address line of the client's shipping address.

shippingAddressLine2.description: Secondary address line of the client's shipping address.

shippingCity.description: City of the client's shipping address.

shippingStateProvince.description: State or province of the client's shipping address.

shippingPostalCode.description: Postal code of the client's shipping address.

shippingCountry.description: Country of the client's shipping address.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the client record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the client record was last updated.

Invoice.title: Invoice
Invoice.description: Represents a generated invoice document, including all its details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Invoice entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserAccount entity that created this invoice. (Relationship: UserAccount 1:N Invoice)

clientId.description: Reference to the Client entity for whom this invoice is created. (Relationship: Client 1:N Invoice)

invoiceNumber.description: Unique identifier or number assigned to the invoice. This can be user-editable or auto-generated for premium subscribers.

issueDate.description: The date when the invoice was issued.

dueDate.description: The date by which the invoice payment is due.

currency.description: The currency code used for all monetary values in the invoice (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

subtotal.description: The sum of all line item totals before tax and discounts.

taxRate.description: The total tax rate percentage applied to the invoice.

taxAmount.description: The total amount of tax applied to the invoice.

discountAmount.description: The total discount amount applied to the invoice.

totalAmount.description: The final total amount of the invoice after applying tax and discounts.

notes.description: Any additional notes or comments included in the invoice.

termsAndConditions.description: Payment terms and conditions specified for the invoice.

senderCompanyName.description: The company name of the invoice sender.

senderAddressLine1.description: The primary address line of the invoice sender.

senderAddressLine2.description: The secondary address line of the invoice sender.

senderCity.description: The city of the invoice sender's address.

senderStateProvince.description: The state or province of the invoice sender's address.

senderPostalCode.description: The postal code of the invoice sender's address.

senderCountry.description: The country of the invoice sender's address.

senderEmail.description: The email address of the invoice sender.

senderPhone.description: The phone number of the invoice sender.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the invoice was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the invoice was last updated.

Estimate.title: Estimate
Estimate.description: Represents a generated estimate document, including all its details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Estimate entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserAccount entity that created this estimate. (Relationship: UserAccount 1:N Estimate)

clientId.description: Reference to the Client entity for whom this estimate is created. (Relationship: Client 1:N Estimate)

estimateNumber.description: Unique identifier or number assigned to the estimate, editable by the user.

issueDate.description: The date when the estimate was issued.

expiryDate.description: The date by which the estimate is valid and can be accepted.

currency.description: The currency code used for all monetary values in the estimate (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR').

subtotal.description: The sum of all line item totals before tax and discounts.

taxRate.description: The total tax rate percentage applied to the estimate.

taxAmount.description: The total amount of tax applied to the estimate.

discountAmount.description: The total discount amount applied to the estimate.

totalAmount.description: The final total amount of the estimate after applying tax and discounts.

notes.description: Any additional notes or comments included in the estimate.

termsAndConditions.description: Terms and conditions specified for the estimate.

senderCompanyName.description: The company name of the estimate sender.

senderAddressLine1.description: The primary address line of the estimate sender.

senderAddressLine2.description: The secondary address line of the estimate sender.

senderCity.description: The city of the estimate sender's address.

senderStateProvince.description: The state or province of the estimate sender's address.

senderPostalCode.description: The postal code of the estimate sender's address.

senderCountry.description: The country of the estimate sender's address.

senderEmail.description: The email address of the estimate sender.

senderPhone.description: The phone number of the estimate sender.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the estimate was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the estimate was last updated.

LineItem.title: LineItem
LineItem.description: Represents a single item or service line within an invoice or an estimate.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LineItem entity.

parentId.description: Reference to the Invoice or Estimate entity this line item belongs to. (Relationship: Invoice 1:N LineItem or Estimate 1:N LineItem)

parentType.description: Indicates whether the `parentId` refers to an 'Invoice' or an 'Estimate'.

description.description: A description of the item or service.

quantity.description: The quantity of the item or service.

unitPrice.description: The price per unit of the item or service.

total.description: The calculated total for the line item (quantity * unitPrice).

orderIndex.description: The display order of this line item within its parent document (invoice or estimate).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the line item was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the line item was last updated.

Subscription.title: Subscription
Subscription.description: Manages details for a user's premium subscription plan.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Subscription entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserAccount entity that holds this subscription. (Relationship: UserAccount 1:1 Subscription)

startDate.description: The date and time when the subscription became active.

endDate.description: The date and time when the subscription is set to expire or end.

pricePerMonth.description: The monthly price of the subscription plan.

paymentProviderSubscriptionId.description: Identifier for the subscription in the external payment provider system (e.g., Stripe, PayPal).

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the subscription record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the subscription record was last updated.

BlogPost.title: BlogPost
BlogPost.description: Represents a blog post published in the integrated blog section, including its content and SEO metadata.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BlogPost entity.

authorId.description: Reference to the UserAccount entity (an admin user) who authored this blog post. (Relationship: UserAccount 1:N BlogPost)

title.description: The main title of the blog post.

slug.description: URL-friendly version of the blog post title, used for permalinks and SEO.

content.description: The main content of the blog post, typically stored in HTML or Markdown format.

outline.description: The AI-generated outline structure saved for future editing.

drafts.description: The AI-generated section drafts saved for future editing.

viewCount.description: The number of times the blog post has been viewed.

excerpt.description: A short summary or snippet of the blog post content, used for listings or meta descriptions.

featuredImageUri.description: URI of the featured image associated with the blog post.

publishDate.description: The date and time when the blog post was published.

status.description: Current status of the blog post (e.g., Draft, Published, Archived).

seoTitle.description: Optimized title for search engine results pages.

seoDescription.description: Meta description for search engines, providing a brief summary of the post content.

seoKeywords.description: Comma-separated keywords relevant to the blog post content for SEO.

categoryIds.description: References to BlogCategory entities that categorize this post. (Relationship: BlogCategory N:N BlogPost)

tagIds.description: References to BlogTag entities associated with this post for further categorization. (Relationship: BlogTag N:N BlogPost)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the blog post was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the blog post was last updated.

GuestInvoice.title: GuestInvoice
GuestInvoice.description: Represents an invoice saved by an anonymous user, identified by their IP address.

ipAddress.description: The IP address of the anonymous user who created the invoice.

GuestEstimate.title: GuestEstimate
GuestEstimate.description: Represents an estimate saved by an anonymous user, identified by their IP address.

ipAddress.description: The IP address of the anonymous user who created the estimate.

CRMContact.title: CRMContact
CRMContact.description: Represents a contact in the CRM system.

CRMLead.title: CRMLead
CRMLead.description: Represents a lead in the CRM system.

EmailSubscriber.title: EmailSubscriber
EmailSubscriber.description: Represents a subscriber to email marketing.

BlogCategory.title: BlogCategory
BlogCategory.description: Defines categories for organizing blog posts.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BlogCategory entity.

name.description: The name of the blog category.

slug.description: URL-friendly version of the category name, used for permalinks.

description.description: A brief description of the blog category.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the blog category was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the blog category was last updated.

BlogTag.title: BlogTag
BlogTag.description: Defines tags for granular classification of blog posts.

id.description: Unique identifier for the BlogTag entity.

name.description: The name of the blog tag.

slug.description: URL-friendly version of the tag name, used for permalinks.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the blog tag was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the blog tag was last updated.

CmsContent.title: CmsContent
CmsContent.description: Stores editable content blocks or sections for the frontend, managed via the CMS backend.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CmsContent entity.

key.description: A unique key that identifies a specific content block (e.g., 'homepage_hero_title', 'about_us_section_text').

title.description: A human-readable title for the content block, used in the CMS for easy identification.

content.description: The actual content of the block, which could be HTML, Markdown, or plain text.

contentType.description: Specifies the format of the content (e.g., HTML, Markdown, PlainText).

lastEditedByUserId.description: Reference to the UserAccount (admin) who last edited this content block. (Relationship: UserAccount 1:N CmsContent)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the CMS content block was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the CMS content block was last updated.

definition.description: Stores core user account information, usage metrics, and subscription status. The document ID `{userId}` MUST match `request.auth.uid` for security. Includes `externalAuthId` which is typically `request.auth.uid`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Stores user-specific default preferences and sender information. The document ID will be the same as `{userId}`. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user, matching `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Contains client records owned by a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns this client.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The unique ID of the client.

definition.description: Stores invoice documents created by a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who created this invoice.

params.name: invoiceId
params.description: The unique ID of the invoice.

definition.description: Stores individual line items for a specific invoice. Includes denormalized 'userId' from the parent invoice for authorization independence. 'parentId' will refer to 'invoiceId'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns the parent invoice.

params.name: invoiceId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent invoice.

params.name: lineItemId
params.description: The unique ID of the line item.

definition.description: Stores estimate documents created by a specific user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who created this estimate.

params.name: estimateId
params.description: The unique ID of the estimate.

definition.description: Stores individual line items for a specific estimate. Includes denormalized 'userId' from the parent estimate for authorization independence. 'parentId' will refer to 'estimateId'.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns the parent estimate.

params.name: estimateId
params.description: The unique ID of the parent estimate.

params.name: lineItemId
params.description: The unique ID of the line item.

definition.description: Manages details for a user's premium subscription plan. The document ID will be the same as `{userId}`. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who owns this subscription.

definition.description: Stores blog posts. Publicly readable for `status: 'Published'`, admin-editable. Includes 'authorId' for audit and 'status' for access control and QAPs.

params.name: blogPostId
params.description: The unique ID of the blog post.

definition.description: Defines categories for organizing blog posts. Publicly readable, admin-editable.

params.name: categoryId
params.description: The unique ID of the blog category.

definition.description: Defines tags for granular classification of blog posts. Publicly readable, admin-editable.

params.name: tagId
params.description: The unique ID of the blog tag.

definition.description: Stores editable content blocks for the frontend. Publicly readable, admin-editable. Includes 'lastEditedByUserId' for audit.

params.name: contentId
params.description: The unique ID of the CMS content block.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for administrative roles (DBAC). A document's existence at this path signifies that the `{userId}` has admin privileges. Used for Authorization Independence by checking existence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique ID of the user who has admin privileges. Matches `request.auth.uid`.

definition.description: Stores invoices created by anonymous users, identified by IP address.

params.name: invoiceId
params.description: The unique ID of the guest invoice.

definition.description: Stores estimates created by anonymous users, identified by IP address.

params.name: estimateId
params.description: The unique ID of the guest estimate.

params.name: contactId
params.description: The unique ID of the CRM contact.

params.name: leadId
params.description: The unique ID of the CRM lead.

params.name: campaignId
params.description: The unique ID of the email campaign.

params.name: subscriberId
params.description: The unique ID of the email subscriber.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for InvoiceFlow is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and clear Access Modeling to ensure security, scalability, and debuggability. All user-specific data is rooted under a top-level `users/{userId}` collection, utilizing path-based ownership. This means that for entities like `UserProfile`, `Client`, `Invoice`, `Estimate`, and `Subscription`, the authenticated user's ID (`request.auth.uid`) can be directly compared with the `{userId}` wildcard in the path, eliminating the need for `get()` calls in security rules to ascertain ownership.

**Authorization Independence (Strategy A):**

1.  **Path-Based Ownership:** For `UserProfile`, `Client`, `Invoice`, `Estimate`, and `Subscription`, the `userId` is an explicit part of the document path (e.g., `users/{userId}/clients/{clientId}`). This directly provides the ownership context for security rules without needing to fetch parent documents.
2.  **Denormalization:** For `LineItem` documents, which are nested under `Invoice` or `Estimate`, the `userId` of the parent invoice/estimate will be denormalized and stored directly within each `lineItem` document. This ensures that even if `LineItem` access were to be evaluated independently, or if a rule needs to confirm the owner of a `lineItem` during a batch write or transaction, it can do so without a `get()` operation on the parent `Invoice` or `Estimate`. This also enables robust querying of line items by user ID if needed. The `userId` property within the `LineItem` document should mirror the `{userId}` from its parent path.
3.  **Global Roles (DBAC):** Admin roles are handled via a dedicated top-level collection `roles_admin/{userId}`. Authorization for admin-only operations (like managing blog posts or CMS content) will check for the existence of a document at `roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid)`, adhering to the 'Existence over Content' principle for global roles and avoiding complex custom claims.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**

1.  **User-Owned Data:** For all personal data collections (e.g., `users/{userId}/clients`, `users/{userId}/invoices`), `list` operations are inherently secure and efficient. A user can only query their own data by construction (`users/{request.auth.uid}/clients`), and the rules will simply verify that `request.auth.uid` matches the `{userId}` in the path. This allows clients to fetch all their relevant data directly without requiring additional server-side filtering or pagination logic in security rules.
2.  **Public Content (Structural Segregation - Strategy B):**
    *   **`blogPosts`:** This collection uses a `status` field (`PUBLISHED`, `DRAFT`) for explicit state modeling. Public users can perform `list` and `get` operations only on documents where `status == 'PUBLISHED'`. Admin users (identified via `roles_admin`) will have full read/write access to all `blogPosts` regardless of status. This segregation by status within a single collection is acceptable for public content where the intent is clear: published items are public, drafts are private to admins. The `list` operation for public users would explicitly filter on `status: 'PUBLISHED'`, which is a Query-as-a-Permission pattern.
    *   **`blogCategories`, `blogTags`, `cmsContent`:** These collections are designed for public read access (allowing unrestricted `list` operations) and admin-only write access. Their homogeneous security posture (all documents are publicly readable, all editable by admins) makes rules simple and `list` operations straightforward without filtering concerns for the public view.
